title: Canon at the Octave, Two Beats Delay
objective: Continue a two-voice canon at the octave: the follower (V1) sings exactly what the leader (V0) sang two beats earlier, an octave lower. The seed gives the leader's first two notes and their echoes.
prompt: Fill both voices through beat 10. Canon constraint: every leader note at beat b must reappear in V1 at beat b+2 transposed down an octave, through the follower's beat 8. The last two beats are a free cadence in both voices (traditional license): close leading-tone to final over 5-1. Composing forward means every new leader note must be consonant with your own note of two beats ago; integer-beat intervals must be consonant and no parallel perfects. Watch sequences: transposing your last two notes up or down by the same interval creates parallel motion with your own echo.
success_criteria: Every row remains strict parser-valid MidGrid.
The given leader and follower openings are unchanged.
V1 equals V0 delayed two beats and transposed down an octave, through beat 8.
Integer-beat intervals are consonant with no parallel perfects.
The final two beats cadence with the leading tone rising to the final.
Corrected solution has no evaluator errors.
